steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence opens with a participial phrase: "Woven from recycled yarn and hand tufted..." Whatever comes in the blank must be the grammatical subject that phrase modifies, plus a linking verb to connect to the adjective phrase "so lush and tactilely inviting..." that follows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the topological tapestries are': The woven, tufted things are the tapestries — making them the subject resolves the modifier correctly, and "are" provides the required linking verb. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the artist has created tapestries that are': The artist was not woven from recycled yarn — this creates a dangling modifier. ✗
  • Choice C — 'topological tapestries': No verb follows before the adjective phrase, leaving the sentence a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — 'the artist's tapestries make them': Again places the artist as the implied actor of the participial phrase — another dangling modifier. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
